message:
🐛 Use stored deploy token for vllm-d CI instead of projected SA token

The projected SA token on ARC ephemeral runners is intermittently
rejected by the API server despite the SA having cluster-admin.
Use a long-lived service-account-token secret stored in GitHub
secrets for reliable auth.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -104,17 +104,16 @@ jobs:
       - name: Set up Helm
         uses: azure/setup-helm@b9e51907a09c216f16ebe8536097933489208112 # v4.3.0
 
-      - name: Configure kubeconfig from SA token
+      - name: Configure kubeconfig
         run: |
           export KUBECONFIG="$RUNNER_TEMP/kubeconfig"
-          SA_TOKEN=$(cat /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token)
-          CA_CERT=/var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/ca.crt
-          kubectl config set-cluster in-cluster \
+          echo "${{ secrets.VLLM_D_CA_CERT }}" > "$RUNNER_TEMP/ca.crt"
+          kubectl config set-cluster vllm-d \
             --server=https://kubernetes.default.svc \
-            --certificate-authority="$CA_CERT" \
+            --certificate-authority="$RUNNER_TEMP/ca.crt" \
             --embed-certs=true
-          kubectl config set-credentials sa-user --token="$SA_TOKEN"
-          kubectl config set-context deploy --cluster=in-cluster --user=sa-user
+          kubectl config set-credentials deployer --token="${{ secrets.VLLM_D_DEPLOY_TOKEN }}"
+          kubectl config set-context deploy --cluster=vllm-d --user=deployer
           kubectl config use-context deploy
           echo "KUBECONFIG=$KUBECONFIG" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
           echo "Verifying cluster access..."
